title: Dryopteris buchananii
taxonomy: Dryopteris buchananii is classified as a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris and family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta and is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales. The class of this fern is Polypodiopsida, and its phylum is Pteridophyta. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae, and the domain is Eukaryota. The overall classification reflects the fern's position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Dryopteris buchananii is a plant species that has been found to have potential medicinal properties, although limited research is available. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective and hepatoprotective activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and to isolate its active compounds.
